'''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、京阪や江戸から[[善光寺]]参拝に向かう道。 #直江津から[[追分宿|追分]]までの北国街道。→[[北国街道 (信越)]] #[[中仙道]]洗馬から[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]]丹波島までの脇往還。→[[北国西街道]]
{{aimai}}

'''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、京阪や江戸から[[善光寺]]参拝に向かう道。 *直江津から[[追分宿|追分]]までの北国街道。→[[北国街道 (信越)]] *[[中仙道]]洗馬から[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]]丹波島までの脇往還。→[[北国西街道]]
{{aimai}}

'''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、京阪や江戸から[[善光寺]]参拝に向かう道。 *[[北国街道 (信越)]] - [[上越市|直江津]]から[[追分宿|追分]]までの北国街道。 *[[北国西街道]] - [[中仙道]]洗馬から[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]]丹波島までの脇往還。
{{Aimai}}

'''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、京阪や江戸から[[善光寺]]参拝に向かう道。 *[[北国街道 (信越)]] - [[上越市|直江津]]から[[追分宿|追分]]までの北国街道。 *[[北国西街道]] - [[中山道]]洗馬から[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]]丹波島までの脇往還。
{{Aimai}}

'''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、正式な街道名ではなく[[善行寺]]参りの参詣者が多いことからの通称名。そのため、複数の街道がこの通称で呼ばれる。 *[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]] : [[追分宿]]（[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]）から[[高田城|高田]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]）までの[[脇往還]]。江戸や関東圏から[[中山道]]経由で追分宿で分岐して善行寺に向かう街道。また、日本海側の地域からは高田城の城下町を起点に南下する。 *[[北国西街道]] : [[洗馬宿]]（長野県[[塩尻市]]）から北国街道[[丹波島宿]]（長野県[[長野市]]）までの脇往還。主に京都・大阪など西の地域から中山道経由で洗馬宿から北上して善行寺に向かう街道。善行寺西街道とも。
{{Aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:せんこうしかいとう}}

'''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、正式な街道名ではなく[[善行寺]]参りの参詣者が多いことからの通称名。そのため、複数の街道がこの通称で呼ばれる。 *[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]] : [[追分宿]]（[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]）から[[高田城|高田]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]）までの[[脇往還]]。江戸や関東圏から[[中山道]]経由で追分宿で分岐して善行寺に向かう街道。また、日本海側の地域からは高田城の城下町を起点に南下する。 *[[北国西街道]] : [[洗馬宿]]（長野県[[塩尻市]]）から[[丹波島宿]]（長野県[[長野市]]）で北国街道に合流する脇往還。主に京都・大阪など西の地域から中山道経由で洗馬宿から北上して善行寺に向かう街道。善行寺西街道とも。
{{Aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:せんこうしかいとう}}

'''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、正式な街道名ではなく[[善行寺]]参りの参詣者が多いことからの通称名。そのため、複数の街道がこの通称で呼ばれる。 *[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]] : [[追分宿]]（[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]）から[[高田城|高田]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]）までの[[脇往還]]。江戸や関東圏から[[中山道]]経由で追分宿で分岐して善行寺に向かう街道。また、日本海側の地域からは高田城の城下町を起点に南下する。 *[[北国西街道]] : [[洗馬宿]]（長野県[[塩尻市]]）から[[丹波島宿]]（長野県[[長野市]]）で北国街道に合流する脇往還。主に京都・大阪など西の地域から中山道経由で洗馬宿から北上して善行寺に向かう街道。善行寺西街道とも。 *[[十日町街道]]・[[谷街道]] : [[小千谷宿]]（[[新潟県]][[小千谷市]]）から[[飯山城|飯山城下]]（[[長野県]][[飯山市]]）で谷街道に接続して、[[松代城|松代城下]]を経由して善行寺に至る街道。主に中越以北の日本海側からの参詣者が利用する。 {{Aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:せんこうしかいとう}}

revision.text:
'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* {{ill|Hokkokukaido (]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects to break]] [[Category:kkk]] [[etsu)|ja|北国街道 (信越)}} : A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} : A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.
* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} : This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route.
{{disambiguation}} [[ja:善光寺街道]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Japanese highways]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Historical roads in Japan]] [[Category:Hh]]

'''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、正式な街道名ではなく[[善行寺]]参りの参詣者が多いことからの通称名。そのため、複数の街道がこの通称で呼ばれる。 *[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]] : [[追分宿]]（[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]）から[[高田城|高田]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]）までの[[脇往還]]。江戸や関東圏から[[中山道]]経由で追分宿で分岐して善行寺に向かう街道。また、日本海側の地域からは高田城の城下町を起点に南下する。 *[[北国西街道]] : [[洗馬宿]]（長野県[[塩尻市]]）から[[丹波島宿]]（長野県[[長野市]]）で北国街道に合流する脇往還。主に京都・大阪など西の地域から中山道経由で洗馬宿から北上して善行寺に向かう街道。善行寺西街道とも。 *[[十日町街道]]・[[谷街道]] : [[小千谷宿]]（[[新潟県]][[小千谷市]]）から[[飯山城|飯山城下]]（[[長野県]][[飯山市]]）で谷街道に接続して、途中の[[福島宿]]（[[須坂市]]）から[[布野の渡し]]で[[千曲川]]を渡り善行寺に至る街道。主に中越以北の日本海側からの参詣者が利用する。 {{Aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:せんこうしかいとう}}

'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* {{ill|Hokkokukaido (Shinetsu)|ja|北国街道 (信越)}} : A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} : A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.
* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} : This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route.

'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* {{ill|Hokkokukaido (Shinetsu)|ja|北国街道 (信越)}} : A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} : A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.
* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} : This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route.

'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* [[:en:Hokkoku_Kaidō|Hokkoku Kaidō]] : A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} : A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.
* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} : This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route.

revision.text:
'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* [[:en:Hokkoku_Kaidō|Hokkoku Kaidō]] : A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} : A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.
* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} : This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route.

* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} ** This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route. {{disambiguation}} [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[ja:善光寺街道]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]]

'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* [[:en:Hokkoku_Kaidō|Hokkoku Kaidō]] ** A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} ** A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. '''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* [[:en:Hokkoku_Kaidō|Hokkoku Kaidō]] ** A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} ** A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.
* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} ** This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route. {{disambiguation}} [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[ja:善光寺街道]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]]

revision.text:
'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* [[:en:Hokkoku_Kaidō|Hokkoku Kaidō]] ** A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} ** A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.
* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} ** This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route. {{disambiguation}} [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[ja:善光寺街道]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]]

'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkoji|ja|善光寺}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:
* [[:en:Hokkoku_Kaidō|Hokkoku Kaidō]] ** A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendo|ja|中山道}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|ja|追分宿}} in {{ill|Karuizawa|ja|軽井沢町}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|ja|高田城}} in {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.
* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道}} ** A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Shiomashuku|ja|洗馬宿}} in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.
* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道}} ** This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿}} in {{ill|Ojiya|ja|小千谷市}}, Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of {{ill|Iiyama Castle|ja|飯山城}} in {{ill|Iiyama|ja|飯山市}}, Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of {{ill|Matsushiro Castle|ja|松代城}} to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route. {{disambiguation}} [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[ja:善光寺街道]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] {{citation needed|Zenkoji Kaido}}[[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]]

revision.text: '''善光寺街道'''（ぜんこうじかいどう）とは、正式な街道名ではなく[[善行寺]]参りの参詣者が多いことからの通称名。そのため、複数の街道がこの通称で呼ばれる。 *[[北国街道 (信越)|北国街道]] : [[追分宿]]（[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]）から[[高田城|高田]]（[[新潟県]][[上越市]]）までの[[脇往還]]。江戸や関東圏から[[中山道]]経由で追分宿で分岐して善行寺に向かう街道。また、日本海側の地域からは高田城の城下町を起点に南下する。 *[[北国西街道]] : [[洗馬宿]]（長野県[[塩尻市]]）から[[丹波島宿]]（長野県[[長野市]]）で北国街道に合流する脇往還。主に京都・大阪など西の地域から中山道経由で洗馬宿から北上して善行寺に向かう街道。善行寺西街道とも。 *[[十日町街道]]・[[谷街道]] : [[小千谷宿]]（[[新潟県]][[小千谷市]]）から[[飯山城|飯山城下]]（[[長野県]][[飯山市]]）で谷街道に接続して、途中の[[福島宿]]（[[須坂市]]）から[[布野の渡し]]で[[千曲川]]を渡り善行寺に至る街道。主に中越以北の日本海側からの参詣者が利用する。 {{Aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:せんこうしかいとう}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:善光寺街道]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11418898]]
Wikidata name: Zenkōji-kaidō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]]. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:

* [[:en:Hokkoku_Kaidō|Hokkoku Kaidō]]
** A branch route of the [[:en:Nakasendō|Nakasendo]], starting at [[:en:Oiwake-shuku|Oiwake-shuku]] in [[:en:Karuizawa, Nagano|Karuizawa]], Nagano Prefecture, and extending to [[:en:Takada Castle|Takada Castle]] in [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Joetsu]], Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.

* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkokunishikaido|qq=|1=Hokkokunishikaido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401310}}
** A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at [[:en:Seba-juku|Shiomashuku]] in [[:en:Shiojiri, Nagano|Shiojiri]], Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbashimashuku|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11418898]]
Wikidata name: Zenkōji-kaidō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkoji]]. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:

* [[:en:Hokkoku_Kaidō|Hokkoku Kaidō]]
** A branch route of the [[:en:Nakasendō|Nakasendo]], starting at [[:en:Oiwake-shuku|Oiwake-shuku]] in [[:en:Karuizawa, Nagano|Karuizawa]], Nagano Prefecture, and extending to [[:en:Takada Castle|Takada Castle]] in [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Joetsu]], Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.

* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkokunishikaido|1=Hokkokunishikaido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401310}}
** A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at [[:en:Seba-juku|Shiomashuku]] in [[:en:Shiojiri, Nagano|Shiojiri]], Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbashimashuku|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.

* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokamachi Highway|1=Tokamachi Highway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133947489}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tani Highway|1=Tani Highway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633097}}
** This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojiya-shuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of [[:en:Iiyama Castle|Iiyama Castle]] in [[:en:Iiyama, Nagano|Iiyama]], Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. It continues via the castle town of [[:en:Matsushiro Castle|Matsushiro Castle]] to Zenkoji. Pilgrims from northern coastal regions, especially from Chūetsu and beyond, commonly used this route.
{{disambiguation}}

* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokamachi Highway|1=Tokamachi Highway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133947489}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tani Highway|1=Tani Highway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633097}} ** This route runs from {{ill|Ojiya-shuku|ja|小千谷宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ojiya-shuku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ojiya, Niigata|Ojiya]], Niigata Prefecture, to the castle town of [[:en:Iiyama Castle|Iiyama Castle]] in [[:en:Iiyama, Nagano|Iiyama]], Nagano Prefecture, where it connects to the Tani Highway. '''Zenkoji Highways''' (善光寺街道) is not an official name for a specific road but a common term used to refer to routes frequently traveled by pilgrims visiting {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkoji|1=Zenkō-ji}}. As such, multiple routes are referred to by this name:

* {{ill|Hokkoku_Kaidō|en|Hokkoku_Kaidō|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}}
** A branch route of the {{ill|Nakasendō|en|Nakasendō|WD=Q1154479|lt=Nakasendo|1=Nakasendō}}, starting at {{ill|Oiwake-shuku|en|Oiwake-shuku|WD=Q3349984|lt=Oiwake-shuku|1=Oiwake-shuku}} in {{ill|Karuizawa, Nagano|en|Karuizawa, Nagano|WD=Q1012064|lt=Karuizawa|1=Karuizawa, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture, and extending to {{ill|Takada Castle|en|Takada Castle|WD=Q10856387|lt=Takada Castle|1=Takada Castle}} in {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Joetsu|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}, Niigata Prefecture. Pilgrims from Edo or the Kanto region would travel via Nakasendo and branch off at Oiwake-shuku to reach Zenkoji. Pilgrims from the coastal regions of the Sea of Japan would start their journey southward from Takada.

* {{ill|Hokkokunishikaido|ja|北国西街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkokunishikaido|1=Q11401310|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11401310}}
** A branch route of the Nakasendo starting at {{ill|Seba-juku|en|Seba-juku|WD=Q3476840|lt=Shiomashuku|1=Seba-juku}} in {{ill|Shiojiri, Nagano|en|Shiojiri, Nagano|WD=Q857272|lt=Shiojiri|1=Shiojiri, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture, and joining the Hokkokukaido at {{ill|Tanbashimashuku|ja|丹波島宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanbashimashuku|qq=}} in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}. This route, also known as Zenkoji West Highway, was primarily used by pilgrims from Kyoto, Osaka, and other western regions traveling via the Nakasendo.

* {{ill|Tokamachi Highway|ja|十日町街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokamachi Highway|1=Q133947489|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133947489}} / {{ill|Tani Highway|ja|谷街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tani Highway|1=Q11633097|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633097}}
